name: Kindness
challenge: Do one kind thing for somebody at school or home, then tap when it is done.

stories.title: The Sharing Star
stories.reflection: How could you share something fairly today?

pages.text: Layan found two biscuits and shared one because sharing feels fair.
Her friend smiled and told a happy story back.
Both felt proud: kindness grows when everybody tries.
Teacher tip for Year 3 writers: evidence for kindness can be a polite word, a fair choice, or a calm solution.
Although sharing can feel tricky at first, it is possible to take turns and still feel happy.

stories.title: Helping Hands
stories.reflection: Who could you help with a small job today?

pages.text: Before dinner, Layan helped tidy toys into a box.
The room looked beautiful and calm again.
Her family felt grateful because teamwork makes hard jobs easier.
If you wrote this as a paragraph in your book, you could open a dictionary to upgrade one word.
